text.title: Modern BA Framework — Integration with Modern Frameworks and Agentic Workflow
text.summary: BABOK integration with modern frameworks, agentic workflow patterns, and prompt templates for the planner and competency-gap subagents.

list.item: SAFe/LeSS — BA competencies mapped to Program Increment planning; BA artifacts feed PI objectives and solution backlogs.
DevOps — requirements in CI/CD pipelines; acceptance criteria as pipeline gates; RTM coverage as a merge check.
Design Thinking — customer-centered requirements; empathy maps and jobs-to-be-done as elicitation outputs feeding BABOK KA-2.
Lean Startup — hypothesis-driven requirements; build-measure-learn cycles as KA-6 evaluation loops.
Data Mesh — domain-driven data requirements; data products as solution artifacts traced in KA-3.

section.title: Agentic Workflow — Planner Subagent

rule.statement: Run a planner subagent that reads project context and emits ba-approach.json before any KA work starts. The planner routes; it does not execute BA work. Human PO/BA sign-off is mandatory before execution.
rule.rationale: Auto-execution skips the only checkpoint where a real PO/BA can correct perspective bias and scope errors before they propagate through the entire pipeline.

section.p: Planner output schema: {perspectives[], knowledge_areas{ka: {include, methodologies[], rationale}}, competencies_needed[], model_routing{task: model}}.
section.reference: Script scaffolding ba-approach.json for a feature

rule.statement: Lock model selection per task in the ba-approach.json. Do not allow cost-driven downgrade at runtime.
rule.rationale: Cost regressions happen when orchestrators downgrade from opus to sonnet for gap analysis; the quality difference is significant and the cost saving is marginal relative to the rework cost.

list.item: Write acceptance criteria → sonnet (requires testing perspective and detail).
Validate requirements with stakeholders → sonnet (needs reasoning and communication planning).
Gap analysis → opus (strategic analysis and trade-off evaluation).
BPMN / process modeling → opus (architecture and complex modeling decisions).

antipattern.description: Using the modern BA framework as a deliverable producer rather than a router.
antipattern.reason: The framework's output is ba-approach.json — a routing decision. Producing "a BA report from the modern framework" creates a document nobody asked for and skips the actual methodology execution.

antipattern.description: KA included in ba-approach.json without a named executable sibling methodology.
antipattern.reason: A KA without a methodology is theatre; the planner must map every INCLUDE'd KA to at least one sibling methodology folder that the executor can load and run.

antipattern.description: Auto-execution from planner to executor without human sign-off on the BA approach.
antipattern.reason: Perspective bias introduced in the planner (e.g., defaulting to Agile for a BPM initiative) propagates through the entire pipeline and produces misaligned artifacts that cannot be corrected without starting over.
